name: one-flew-over-the-cuckoos-nest
description: Ken Kesey's classic American novel about a mental institution, the tyrannical Nurse Ratched, and the free-spirited Randle McMurphy who challenges her authority — a powerful allegory for conformity, individuality, and institutional power.
useCase: Explore one of the most influential American novels of the 20th century — a story about power, rebellion, and the cost of being free.
knownRisks: The novel contains racial slurs, sexual content, and graphic depictions of institutional violence that reflect its 1962 publication context.
The portrayal of mental illness and psychiatric treatment reflects 1960s understanding, not modern standards.
Chief Bromden's narrative as a Native American character has been critiqued as inauthentic (written by a white author).
